name: Ice Cream
description: Cones, sundaes, scoops, and Ben & Jerry's — the cold history of frozen treats.

questions.q: What flavor was the first commercially sold ice cream in the United States?

source.notes: Still the bestselling flavor in the U.S. today.

questions.q: Which First Lady is credited with making ice cream a White House dessert staple in the early 1800s?

source.notes: She famously served strawberry ice cream at her husband's 1813 inauguration ball.

questions.q: Which U.S. president had over 200 ice cream recipes and is sometimes called America's first ice cream connoisseur?

source.notes: His handwritten vanilla ice cream recipe is held by the Library of Congress.

questions.q: Who patented the first hand-cranked ice cream maker in 1843?

source.notes: Philadelphia inventor; her hand-crank double-cylinder design was the basis for home machines for over 100 years.

questions.q: At what World's Fair was the ice cream cone reportedly popularized in 1904?

source.notes: Syrian-American Ernest Hamwi rolled a waffle into a cone for an ice-cream vendor whose dishes ran out.

questions.q: Which country consistently ranks at or near the top in per-capita ice cream consumption?

source.notes: About 28 liters per person per year — close to the U.S.

questions.q: Which Ben & Jerry's flavor is named after a Grateful Dead member and combines cherry ice cream with fudge flakes?

source.notes: Named after Jerry Garcia.

questions.q: Which Ben & Jerry's flavor is named after a Vermont jam band?

source.notes: Chocolate ice cream with marshmallow swirls, caramel swirls, and fudge fish.

questions.q: Which Ben & Jerry's flavor was named after Stephen Colbert?

source.notes: Vanilla ice cream with fudge-covered waffle cone pieces and caramel.

questions.q: Which Ben & Jerry's flavor was inspired by Jimmy Fallon and his Tonight Show?

source.notes: Caramel and chocolate ice cream with cookie dough swirls. ('Late Night Snack' is also a Fallon flavor.)

questions.q: Which classic three-flavor combo of vanilla, chocolate, and strawberry is named after an Italian city?

source.notes: Named after Naples (Napoli) — colors mirror the Italian flag.

questions.q: Which Italian three-flavor frozen dessert often features pistachio, cherry, and chocolate?

source.notes: From the Italian 'spuma' = foam.

questions.q: How does Italian gelato differ from American ice cream?

source.notes: Gelato uses more milk than cream and is churned slowly to limit air.

questions.q: What is the typical minimum butterfat percentage for U.S. ice cream to be labeled 'ice cream' (FDA)?
When is U.S. National Ice Cream Day celebrated?

source.notes: Established by President Reagan in 1984. July is National Ice Cream Month.

questions.q: Which 1903 inventor patented the Popsicle (frozen-on-a-stick) concept after accidentally leaving fruit-flavored soda outside?

source.notes: Then 11 years old. Patented in 1924.

questions.q: Who invented the chocolate-coated Good Humor bar in 1920 and started the ice cream truck industry?

source.notes: Burt added a stick to chocolate-dipped ice cream and put bells on his trucks.

questions.q: What is a 'sundae'?
choices: Ice cream with toppings (sauces, nuts, fruit)

source.notes: Said to have been invented in 1881; spelling 'sundae' likely arose from blue-law restrictions on selling 'soda' on Sundays.

questions.q: Which 16th-century Italian-born French queen is credited with introducing 'gelato' to France?

source.notes: Brought Italian chefs and gelato when she married Henry II.

questions.q: What is the iconic Häagen-Dazs name's origin?

source.notes: Founders Reuben & Rose Mattus invented it in 1961 to evoke Old World craftsmanship; it has no actual meaning in Danish.
